name: Halo
primary_sources: Official History of the Chin Dynasty

distinctive_features: 22° halo with a large ring around the Sun or Moon
light pillars appearing as vertical columns
sun dogs appearing as bright spots on either side of the Sun
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: radius of about 22° for the 22° halo
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: rings, arcs, and spots in the sky

domain_specific_visual.CIRRUS_CLOUDS: typically seen in cirrus or cirrostratus clouds

functional_attributes.primary_function: optical display caused by light refraction and reflection

domain_specific_functional.WEATHER_INDICATOR: indicates approaching frontal systems and potential rain
domain_specific_functional.CULTURAL_OMEN: considered an omen of bad weather in some cultures

relationships.description: Halos are caused by the interaction of light with ice crystals.
Halos are often seen in cirrus or cirrostratus clouds.

contextual_attributes.origin: Ancient Greek, Chinese, and European observations
contextual_attributes.geographic_range: global, wherever ice crystals are present in the atmosphere
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: used in weather lore and as omens in various cultures
primary_sources: Official History of the Chin Dynasty

contrastive_constraints: NOT purely decorative or artistic representation
